items.summary: Brent crude futures rose $1.93, or 2% to $96 by 0011 GMT, the highest ‌since June 8. It had settled over $3 higher at $94.07 in the previous session, just shy of a six-week high.

items.title: Musk leaves door open to Tesla-SpaceX merger
items.summary: Elon Musk has suggested a possible merger between Tesla and SpaceX, citing increasing overlap.  Investors now believe a combination of these companies is highly probable within years.  Tesla already supplies batteries and manufacturing technologies for SpaceX projects.  The companies are also jointly developing a semiconductor manufacturing facility for AI chips.  However, regulatory approvals and governance complexities present significant hurdles to any such transaction.

items.title: Trump’s 200% generic tariff threat would imperil Americans: Indian pharma
items.summary: Effective August 1, 2026, all generic drugs being brought into the United States will continue to have a tariff of zero per cent for a two-year period of time, after which the tariff will be raised to 100% for a one-year period of time, and 200% thereafter, said Donald Trump

items.title: Bengal govt to initiate process of listing West Bengal Power Development Corporation on stock exchanges
items.summary: Addressing the Assembly, Adhikari said listing of WBPDCL would strengthen it financially, improve its transparency, generate employment and enhance spending under corporate social responsibility.

items.title: Nestle India extends rally on stellar June quarter, higher costs may limit upside
items.summary: Nestle India shares reached a record high after a strong June quarter performance.  Revenue growth accelerated significantly year-on-year, reaching 25.2 percent.  However, operating margins moderated sequentially due to increased advertising investments.  Exports also showed steady growth, driven by market penetration and product additions.  The company anticipates continued healthy demand supported by premiumization and innovation.

items.title: Benchmark indices fall amid rising crude oil prices and West Asia conflict
items.summary: India's benchmark indices ended lower on Wednesday, as crude oil prices increased. The Nifty50 and Sensex experienced significant drops, falling below key levels. Midcap and smallcap stocks also saw declines, reflecting broad market weakness. Most sector indices ended in negative territory, except for FMCG and Auto. Brent crude oil prices rose, impacting investor sentiment across the market.

items.title: Stocks in news: Infosys, IndusInd Bank, Eternal, Adani Green, Dr Reddy's
items.summary: Indian markets experienced a decline on Wednesday due to global and geopolitical concerns. IndusInd Bank reported a significant profit increase while Eternal saw substantial net profit growth. BPCL, however, posted a considerable loss in the first quarter. Adani Green announced a moderate profit rise, and Dr Reddy's profit declined. Several other companies like Infosys will announce their first quarter results today.

items.title: D-St set for a negative opening as GIFT Nifty signals weak start
items.summary: Domestic equities declined as broad-based selling persisted across sectors. India VIX surged, reflecting heightened market volatility and investor fear. Analysts expect sideways trading with a negative bias due to global tensions. Foreign portfolio investors were net sellers, impacting market sentiment negatively. The Indian rupee fell to a two-month low as oil prices climbed.

items.title: IOB wipes out  ₹20,000-cr accumulated losses, to raise  ₹5,000 cr in share sale
items.summary: The lender reported a robust performance in the June quarter, with net profit rising 49.32% year-on-year to  ₹1,659 crore, while net interest income (NII) increased 34.3% to  ₹3,688 crore. Operating profit grew 14.21% to  ₹2,693 crore.

items.title: CAFE hits a new flashpoint as carmakers split on easy route for laggards
items.summary: India's auto sector is split over a proposal to let companies that miss fuel-efficiency targets buy compliance credits directly from the regulator at a flat rate. The rift threatens to delay changes to the current CAFE rules and marks the latest policy divide within the industry.

items.title: West Asia war pushes ONGC to plan India's first strategic gas reserve
items.summary: A strategic natural gas reserve can cushion the impact of crises such as the West Asia war, which has disrupted global energy supplies

items.title: BusinessNext raises $40 million from ServiceNow for SEA and Australia expansion
items.summary: BusinessNext has raised $40 million from ServiceNow Ventures, valuing the Noida-based banking software firm at $700 million—up from $181 million in 2021. The deal gives ServiceNow a 5% stake and opens BusinessNext's access to Southeast Asia, New Zealand and Australia.

items.title: Probe stalls transfer of Ratan Tata’s Tata Sons shares to his foundations
items.summary: Ratan Tata's share transfer to charitable foundations is halted following a complaint regarding ownership, leading to a regulatory probe. The executors of Tata’s will are unable to proceed with the transfer until the charity commissioner provides a verdict on the allegations.

items.title: When pay ceiling catches up with IT firms' stock-rich CEOs
items.summary: Stock awards accumulated over years are set to lift CEOs' reported pay above the statutory remuneration ceiling at some IT services firms, calling for shareholder nod. While the firms say it is only about accounting, investors are increasingly scrutinizing executive compensation and disclosures.

items.title: Trump's 200% generic drug tariff threat puts $9.7 billion Indian pharma exports at risk; industry says reshoring not practical
items.summary: US President Trump's proposed tariffs on imported generic drugs will not immediately affect Indian drugmakers.  Indian companies export significant generic medicines to the United States market.  The proposed tariffs are set to begin in 2028, offering a two-year window.  This lead time allows Indian firms to explore alternative export markets and adapt.  American patients and insurers will likely face increased healthcare costs after tariffs are imposed.

items.title: Top retailers raise Rs 4,000 crore as store expansion hits four-year high
items.summary: India's largest retailers are significantly increasing offline store launches this fiscal year. Companies like Reliance Retail and Trent are raising substantial funds for expansion. This growth marks the fastest pace seen since the Covid-19 pandemic period. Retailers are investing in physical stores and omni-channel infrastructure. Expansion efforts are focused on reaching deeper into existing markets.

items.title: Engineering students flock to electronics as chip, AI boom grows
items.summary: Indian engineering colleges see a surge in electronics course applications this year. This trend is driven by new processor architectures and industry demands. Government semiconductor initiatives are also fueling student interest in hardware. Core engineering branches are gaining popularity alongside computer science programs. Companies are actively hiring graduates with interdisciplinary hardware and software skills.

items.title: Blackstone ties up with HDFC Ergo Ex-MD Anuj Tyagi for new insurance biz
items.summary: Blackstone and Anuj Tyagi are launching a new general insurance company in India. This venture will be 90% foreign-owned, following recent FDI limit increases. The company has filed an R1 application with IRDAI for approval to operate. It plans to utilize generative and agentic AI across all its operations. This move signifies growing global investor interest in India's insurance sector.

items.title: Banks to tap 25 OEMs in battle against AI-led cybersecurity threats
items.summary: Indian banks are identifying twenty-five original equipment manufacturers for AI risk mitigation partnerships.  A working group is developing a framework to identify and strengthen safeguards against cyberattacks.  Banks will reduce network attack surface area by deploying defensive AI agents.  Internal controls may not prevent exposure through weak vendors or third-party components.  Vulnerabilities in embedded code can spread flaws across multiple financial institutions.

items.title: Adani seeks clearance to own airline
items.summary: Adani Group seeks to dilute a clause preventing airport operators from holding airline stakes. This change would enable the conglomerate to launch its own airline operations. The government is consulting legal experts on amending the existing airport privatization agreements. Such a move could introduce competition into the domestic aviation market. However, rivals anticipate strong opposition and regulatory scrutiny over potential advantages.

items.title: Pharma stocks slide as Trump's 100% generic drug tariff plan spooks investors
items.summary: Indian drug shares declined Wednesday following US President Trump's tariff announcement.  These proposed tariffs will significantly impact companies with substantial US revenue exposure.  Analysts suggest investors should now focus on domestically oriented Indian pharmaceutical firms.  The Nifty Pharma index saw a notable decrease, while the benchmark Nifty 50 also fell.  Negotiations are expected given India's vital role in supplying affordable medicines to the US.

items.title: European Union gives its greenlight to Paramount and Warners mega merger with some conditions
items.summary: European Union gives its greenlight to Paramount and Warner's mega merger with some conditions

items.title: India awaits US' final action in forced labour trade probe
items.summary: Greer told the Senate Finance Committee that the investigations examined the top 60 US trading partners for failing to adopt and effectively enforce import bans on goods produced with forced labour. "As soon as tomorrow, USTR will release its final responsive action on its Section 301 investigation of our top 60 trading partners' failure to address international trade in forced goods at their borders," he said.

items.title: ET Graphics: India is winning the battle against hunger, but not food costs
items.summary: India has achieved a 42% decline in undernourished people over two decades.  The number of Indians unable to afford a healthy diet also fell significantly.  This progress outpaces global trends in tackling hunger and malnutrition.  However, the cost of a healthy diet has risen by over a third.  These findings are based on a new report by the UN's Food and Agriculture Organization.

items.title: Mauritius tax protocol to give Indian taxman more powers to probe offshore entities
items.summary: Mauritius cabinet ratified a tax protocol granting Indian officials more questioning powers.  This allows direct challenges to offshore entities suspected of tax evasion.  The principal purpose test will deny treaty benefits if tax avoidance is a primary aim.  This change follows the Tiger Global verdict and strengthens genuine substance requirements.  The protocol aims to ensure treaty benefits are for legitimate commercial purposes.

items.title: Paramount secures EU nod for $110 billion Warner Bros deal, US hurdles ahead
items.summary: WARNER-BROS-DIS-M-A-PARAMOUNT-SKYDAN-EU:Paramount secures EU nod for $110 billion Warner Bros deal, US hurdles ahead

items.title: BCCI hikes reserve price for its title rights by over 15%
items.summary: The BCCI has significantly raised its reserve prices for title and associate sponsorship rights.  New bids are now invited for these valuable commercial properties.  The title sponsorship cycle runs until March 2028, covering many international matches.  This move aligns with other BCCI commercial deals due for renewal.  The board seeks to maximize revenue from its key sporting assets.

items.title: Solar cell makers flag investor jitters over sourcing rule relief
items.summary: India extended a solar cell sourcing exemption for certain projects by seven months. Local manufacturers express concerns about this decision impacting investor confidence and production planning. The exemption allows open access and net metering projects to use imported solar cells. This move aims to protect standalone module makers and support domestic capacity growth. The government maintains its commitment to promoting local solar manufacturing.

items.title: Bollywood fades, Bridgerton shines as global OTT shows changing India’s jewellery taste
items.summary: Global streaming shows now dictate Indian jewellery preferences, replacing Bollywood's influence. Young shoppers seek designs inspired by "Bridgerton" and "Emily in Paris" series. Rising gold prices also encourage consumers towards lighter, more wearable diamond jewellery. Millennials and Gen Z view jewellery as personal style and self-expression. This shift reshapes India's significant diamond jewellery market.

items.title: India's 7,000-km bullet rail plan gets homegrown signalling push
items.summary: India is developing its own high-speed rail signalling protocol, the Bharat Train Control System. This system will be tailored for Indian conditions and reduce foreign technology dependence. Designs and route plans for future bullet-train corridors are expected by mid-2027. The new protocol will be open-source and cost-effective for nationwide deployment. Domestic suppliers will benefit from the country's own high-speed train operation protocols.

items.title: MTNL liabilities jump to ₹40,000 crore in FY26, but firm remains Ebitda positive:  Jyotiraditya Scindia
items.summary: Mahanagar Telephone Nigam Ltd's liabilities will reach ₹40,000 crore by 2025-26.  The company holds non-core assets valued at approximately ₹50,000 crore.  Government measures have ensured MTNL remains Ebitda positive since 2020-21.  Its Ebitda is projected to steadily improve over the next few years.  This financial and administrative support aids in managing company liabilities.

items.title: Batista Brothers Clinch Venezuela Oil Stake as Deals Gain Steam
items.summary: Brazil’s billionaire Batista brothers have acquired a stake in an oil production project in Venezuela, the latest deal to emerge in a country that the Trump administration is vowing to reinvigorate after years of sanctions.

items.title: JSW Infrastructure eyes 127 MT cargo in FY27 even as Fujairah terminal disruption continues
items.summary: JSW Infrastructure anticipates handling 127 million tonnes of cargo this fiscal year.  Operations at its Fujairah liquid terminal are gradually returning to normal.  Eight storage tanks will be operational by late July or early August.  The company is also proceeding with significant domestic expansion plans.  Profit for the June quarter saw a slight decrease compared to last year.

items.title: Dr Reddy’s aims to resume semaglutide supply by November after API fix
items.summary: Dr Reddy's expects to resume semaglutide supplies by November after resolving quality issues. The company identified the root cause and plans corrective measures by September. Financial results were impacted by a provision linked to the semaglutide active pharmaceutical ingredient. Despite disruptions, Dr Reddy's maintains its growth and profitability guidance for fiscal year 2027. The drugmaker anticipates supplying millions of semaglutide units this fiscal year.

items.title: Tata Communications flags role cuts, takes Rs 44.78 cr charge
items.summary: Tata Communications is restructuring its organization, incurring a one-time staff cost optimization charge. The company reported a decline in consolidated net profit due to restructuring expenses and a data centre outage. Revenue rose by 10.5% from a year earlier, while EBITDA increased. The company remains focused on accelerating growth across its portfolios. Management believes this is a forward-looking effort to align talent.

items.title: Govt aims to double food processing level to 2% over next five years: Chirag Paswan
items.summary: India plans to double its food processing level to twenty-five percent within five years.  This ambitious goal follows significant reforms implemented over the past twelve years.  The government views food processing as a priority sector for national economic growth.  Various schemes are actively supporting micro and large food processing enterprises.  Connecting every farm to a processing unit is a key future objective.

items.title: Maize gains ground in M.P., may offset decline in Karnataka, Maharashtra
items.summary: Overall, the country’s maize acreage has dipped about 5%, raising some eyebrows in the livestock feed scene

items.title: US tariff threat drags Nifty Pharma 1.3% lower at close; Lupin, PPL Pharma lead decline
items.summary: Only two of the index’s 20 constituents ended in positive territory, while 18 declined

items.title: Lower area under arhar in India’s Karnataka unlikely to be offset elsewhere
items.summary: This is despite higher coverage in U.P., Telangana and Andhra; Maharashtra and M.P. report double-digit decline

items.title: FCNR (B) deposits: Small, mid-sized private banks reach out to GIFT City IBUs to offer leverage to NRIs
items.summary: This initiative aims to provide non-resident Indian (NRI) customers with access to leverage, enabling them to place higher deposits with tenors of 3-5 years.

items.title: Nestle India Q1 results: Consolidated net profit surges 48% to ₹959 crore
items.summary: Consolidated revenue from operations increased 25.16% year-on-year to ₹6,378.18 crore.

items.title: Rain deficit shrinks to 21 % as wet spell intensifies in West, North-West
items.summary: Central India (deficit of 13 per cent) and North-West India (18 per cent) emerge as best-performing regions this week

items.title: IndusInd Bank aims for 1% RoA by FY27-end; focus on growing retail book
items.summary: The private-sector lender posted a consolidated net profit of  ₹1,037 crore for the June quarter, up 72% on year and 75% on quarter, largely led by a decline in operating expenses and provisions.

items.title: ‘Seriously evaluating very large renewable energy sites’:  Adani Green after success in Gujarat’s Khavda
items.summary: Total income during the quarter rose 16% year-on-year to ₹4,663 crore, while revenue from power supply increased 29% to ₹4,280 crore

items.title: Net FDI turned negative again in May 2026 with outflows exceeding inflows by $74 million
items.summary: RBI data shows that, even though outflows of direct investment slowed in May 2026, inflows slowed even more. This snapped a three-month streak of positive net inflows.

items.title: Indian seamless pipe manufactures add USD 1.4 bn in forex annually: Industry body
items.summary: Indian seamless pipe manufacturers contribute USD 1.4 billion annually in foreign exchange. This significant contribution comes from import substitution and overseas sales of made-in-India products. Domestic companies now produce critical seamless pipes previously imported, saving millions each year. Exports of these specialized pipes also add substantially to the nation's earnings. The industry's growth aligns with the vision of making India a global manufacturing hub.

items.title: India’s April-June LPG imports lowest in 8 years
items.summary: LPG imports fell 45% year-on-year to 2.85 million tonnes in the April-June quarter, the lowest for the period in eight years, as disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz curbed supplies.

items.title: Gold, silver smuggling surges after Indian govt more than doubles import duty
items.summary: Data provided in the Lok Sabha show over 186% rise in gold and 10% increase in silver

items.title: Ayr Energy counters Zetwerk over transformer tech dispute in US ITC dispute
items.summary: Ayr alleged that an individual joined the company under false pretences, gained access to confidential information, and later joined Zetwerk to lead a newly established transformer business.

items.title: Higher power demand, volumes lift Adani Power FY27 Q1 profit by 42%
items.summary: Company’s revenue from operations also increased 34 percent year-on-year to ₹18,902 crore during the quarter

items.title: Dr Reddy’s net profit falls 69% in Q1; semaglutide supplies to resume by November
items.summary: Dr Reddy’s attributed the weak quarterly performance to lower lenalidomide revenues and an unexpected setback related to semaglutide API

items.title: UltraTech Cement comes up with a record June quarter, profit surges 17%
items.summary: Demand pipeline helps as revenues rise 16%; Ebitda strong despite war-led price hikes. UltraTech Cement achieved its highest-ever June quarter volume and profit. The company's volume growth exceeded industry expectations, indicating market share gains. Despite rising costs, Ebitda increased, demonstrating strong cost absorption capabilities.

items.title: Satcom set to play a transformative role; opportunities ahead vast: Trai Chairman
items.summary: Satellite communications will expand seamless connectivity across India.  This technology will support strategic areas and bridge the digital divide.  India's space economy is projected to grow significantly by 2030.  New ventures reflect rapid private participation and innovation in the space sector.  Agile policy frameworks and collaboration will drive technological advancement and inclusive growth.

items.title: Hindustan Petroleum posts net loss of ₹12,265 crore impacted by West Asia crisis
items.summary: The Mumbai-based refiner’s revenue increased about 21% on a year-over-year basis to ₹1.45 lakh crore in the reported quarter

items.title: UTI AMC Q1 profit rises 24% to ₹294 cr on lower costs
items.summary: Revenue increases 7% to ₹584 crore from ₹547 crore a year ago

items.title: India creates ₹40,000 crore corpus for coal mine closure
items.summary: Targets scientific closure of 147 mines, boosting restoration, communities and sustainable land reuse

items.title: Less paperwork, fixed timelines to make solar rooftop installations simpler in Delhi
items.summary: Delhi has simplified rooftop solar regulations to encourage domestic adoption. The process now involves two steps, reducing paperwork and eliminating registration fees. Technical feasibility analysis is waived for systems up to 10 kW capacity. Discoms must complete feasibility within fifteen days or it is deemed granted. Installation of rooftop plants will now be completed within twenty-five days.

items.title: Sona Comstar to form 2 JVs with Japanese Denso Corp for electric, hybrid powertrain systems
items.summary: Sona Comstar and DENSO Corporation are forming two joint ventures for electric and hybrid powertrain systems. These partnerships will focus on solutions for four-wheelers and larger vehicle applications. Another venture will develop systems for electric and hybrid two- and three-wheelers. The companies aim to build scalable and cost-efficient advanced electric and hybrid solutions. These joint ventures will be anchored in India and meet global standards.

items.title: IndusInd Bank posts 72% rise in Q1 profit as asset quality improves
items.summary: Despite modest growth in net interest income and continued pressure on margins, IndusInd Bank expects net interest margins to improve in the second half of FY27, with loan growth matching industry trends

items.title: Eternal’s consolidated net profit soars to ₹92 crore, NOV jumps by 56 per cent
items.summary: The company said that 200 net new dark stores were added during the quarter taking the total count to 2,443. Meanwhile, District’s NOV growth accelerated to 60 per cent year-on-year to Rs ₹3,218 crore

items.title: CSB Bank Q1 profit jumps 27% YoY to Rs 150 crore as provisions decline
items.summary: CSB Bank reported a twenty-seven percent year-on-year net profit increase for the June quarter. Lower provisions and improved net interest income aided the bank's financial performance. Sequential profit declined due to typical seasonal business trends in the first quarter. Gold loans saw significant growth, while total advances also expanded. The bank focuses on customer acquisition and retail growth after technology upgrades.

items.title: Anurag Jain appointed CEO of NITI Aayog
items.summary: Jain is an IAS officer of Madhya Pradesh cadre, 1989 batch

items.title: After equities and mutual funds, it’s time for bond SIPs
items.summary: A handful of OBPPs, including IndiaBonds and Grip Invest, have introduced Bond SIPs to simplify investing in fixed income

items.title: Tariff hike by US will only increase drug prices: Dr Reddy's CEO
items.summary: US tariff increases on generic medicines will lead to higher drug prices in India. Dr Reddy's Laboratories CEO Erez Israeli stated relocation to the US is impractical. The company will observe evolving situations without taking immediate special actions. Additional costs are anticipated due to the proposed tariff changes. Partnerships and contract manufacturing in the US remain possibilities if beneficial.

items.title: Total flow of financial resources to the commercial sector zoomed 148% to ₹7.73 lakh cr in Q1FY27
items.summary: With interest rates in the bond market tightening, corporates tapped banks for credit in a big way in the first three months of current

items.title: Homegrown beverage brand Maaza completes 50 years
items.summary: Maaza, a Coca-Cola India mango beverage, marked its 50th anniversary and National Mango Day. The Department of Posts released a special cover honouring farmers for their vital contributions. Coca-Cola reiterated its commitment to farmers through Project Unnati for sustainable practices. This initiative helps farmers nearly double their per-acre yields significantly. Maaza achieved billion-dollar brand status in 2024, joining Coca-Cola's elite portfolio.

items.title: HPCL posts net loss of ₹12,265 Cr as West Asia conflict impacts margins
items.summary: In the year-ago period, HPCL had reported a consolidated net profit of roughly Rs ₹4,111 crore. During Q4 FY26, its profits stood at around Rs ₹6,065 crore.

items.title: Indian economy navigated well external uncertainties: RBI Bulletin
items.summary: “Industrial and services sector indicators remained firm. Farm sector is witnessing uneven southwest monsoon, but the impact on food inflation may be mitigated by comfortable foodgrain stocks.”

items.title: Dr Reddy’s reports muted Q1FY27 results, net profit drops 69%
items.summary: Dr Reddy’s missed estimates, with revenue down 5.6% to  ₹8,071 crore, and profit falling to  ₹444 crore.

items.title: DailyObjects eyes ₹400 cr revenue in FY27, plans 150 stores over five years
items.summary: DailyObjects is also looking at international expansion, with the US emerging as an early focus market, says CEO

items.title: Samsung launches its priciest smartphone lineup ever amid chip cost crunch
items.summary: Soaring raw material prices have made the Galaxy Z Fold 8 series Samsung's most expensive range yet, even as the company works to buffer consumers from the full brunt of chip inflation.

items.title: Intel layoffs: Chipmaker to begin fresh round of job cuts in data centre division
items.summary: Intel Layoffs: The company added that the organisational changes are expected to help it operate with greater ‘simplicity and speed.’

items.title: Foreign banks show strongest rate transmission in current RBI easing cycle: Bulletin
items.summary: Foreign banks showed strongest policy easing transmission during the rate-cut cycle. They lowered lending rates more sharply than other bank types. Deposit rates also saw steeper reductions from foreign banks. The Reserve Bank of India reduced its repo rate by 1.25 percentage points. This easing cycle occurred between February 2025 and May 2026.

items.title: Mark Cuban sounds alarm over OpenAI's AI spending, ‘trillion dollars spent just to be an app’
items.summary: Billionaire investor Mark Cuban criticises OpenAI's $1 trillion investment plan, questioning its potential for adequate returns.&nbsp;

items.title: Ahead of Market: 10 things that will decide stock market action on Thursday
items.summary: Indian stock markets experienced significant declines on Wednesday. Escalating global tensions and inflation concerns spooked investors, leading to broad market weakness. Rate-sensitive sectors like realty and banking saw considerable losses. However, FMCG and auto stocks showed relative resilience due to strong earnings. US and European markets also faced mixed trading ahead of earnings.

items.title: Samsung to select 100 teams in India in the next phase of innovation, education challenge
items.summary: Samsung's innovation competition will select one hundred Indian teams for further evaluation.  These teams will receive mentorship and expert guidance after submitting their ideas.  The top four teams will receive incubation grants totaling two crore rupees.  This initiative has reached thousands of students across one hundred thirty-five cities.  The program empowers young people to develop technology-based community solutions.

items.title: Market Trading Guide: Bajaj Auto among 7 stock recommendations for Thursday
items.summary: Indian markets ended lower amid geopolitical tensions and inflation concerns, but analysts remain bullish on select stocks. Seven recommendations, including Bajaj Auto, Bosch and Asian Paints, highlight strong technical setups and continuation patterns, suggesting potential upside despite broader market volatility and cautious investor sentiment.

items.title: UCO Bank Q1 net up 8% at ₹656 crore on higher operating profit and lower provisions
items.summary: The bank’s net interest income  rose to ₹2808.26 crore from ₹1403.23 crore for the corresponding period last fiscal.

items.title: Titagarh-Ramkrishna Forgings set to start forged railway wheel production in August
items.summary: The plant will end Indian Railways’ decades-long dependence on imported forged wheels

items.title: Sikkim tunnel blast tragedy: NHPC announces Rs 5 lakh compensation to family of each deceased
items.summary: NHPC announced Rs 5 lakh compensation for each deceased worker in a Sikkim tunnel blast.  The explosion at the Teesta Stage-VI hydro power plant killed at least twenty workers.  Rescue operations are continuing round-the-clock for the remaining trapped personnel.  Thirteen bodies have been retrieved so far from the collapsed tunnel.  NHPC expressed profound grief and extended condolences to the bereaved families.

items.title: HFCL to set up Rs 215-crore data centre connectivity products factory
items.summary: HFCL's board approved a Rs 215 crore investment for a new manufacturing facility. This plant will produce advanced data centre connectivity products for AI infrastructure. The company anticipates significant global demand for these specialized components. This new unit is expected to be commissioned by September 2027. HFCL aims to meet growing needs in data centers and cloud computing.

items.title: Adani Green Q1 profit rises about 20% as clean energy capacity crosses 20 GW
items.summary: To reduce the uncertainty in its earnings, the renewable energy arm of the Adani Group signed a pact with the power distribution arm of the group, Adani Energy Solutions Ltd, to supply 4 GW power.

items.title: TVS Motor indicates it may separate TVS Credit business at ‘appropriate time’
items.summary: TVS Credit reported a total Income of ₹1,918 crores for Q1 FY27, a growth of 13% from Q1 FY26, and a net profit of ₹208 crores for Q1 FY27, a growth of 15% compared to the same period last year

items.title: Liquid cooling to see fastest adoption among neoclouds and sovereign AI projects: Dell
items.summary: Dell Technologies India, discuss the company’s AI strategy, enterprise adoption trends and India’s growing role in the AI infrastructure landscape.

items.title: Maharashtra releases first beneficiary list under farm loan waiver scheme
items.summary: Eligible farmers will receive SMS alerts for Aadhaar authentication before the loan waiver amount is credited to their accounts through DBT

items.title: IndusInd bank’s Q1FY27 PAT up 47% on lower provisions
items.summary: Bank sees lower costs and provisions for bad loans

items.title: MTNL liabilities reach Rs 40,000 cr in FY26; no restructuring plan in work: Jyotiraditya Scindia
items.summary: MTNL's liabilities have reached approximately forty thousand crore rupees by fiscal year 2025-26.  Government measures have helped MTNL become operationally profitable and EBITDA-positive.  These actions include debt restructuring and financial support for employee retirement schemes.  MTNL holds non-core assets valued higher than its total outstanding liabilities.  No new restructuring plans for MTNL are being considered by the government.

items.title: Holding transport fuels, LPG prices pushes BPCL into red; posts net loss of ₹1,873 cr
items.summary: Consolidated total expenses were also higher during the June quarter at ₹1.66 lakh crore

items.title: Temasek bets on India’s next decade, signals fresh $10 billion push despite portfolio dip
items.summary: India’s share in Temasek’s global portfolio declined in FY26 following a blockbuster Schneider Electric exit, but the country retained its position as the Singapore investment giant’s best performing market over the decade.

items.title: Top OMCs post losses with government weighing relief measures
items.summary: State-run oil companies HPCL and BPCL reported significant losses for the April-June quarter.  This occurred as they sold petrol, diesel, and LPG below market rates.  Elevated crude prices, driven by West Asia conflict, impacted their earnings.  The oil ministry is now working on a compensation proposal for these companies.  The companies' financial outlook depends on global oil price trajectory and potential government support.

items.title: Godrej Capital enters gold loan business with Kanakadurga Finance acquisition
items.summary: Godrej Capital acquired Kanakadurga Finance's gold loan business to expand its consumer finance segment. This acquisition aims to build a Rs 1 lakh crore assets under management franchise by 2031. Kanakadurga Finance's business adds 54 branches and 250 employees to Godrej Capital. The gold loan segment is experiencing significant growth due to rising gold prices. Godrej Capital also launched its wealth management business earlier this year.

items.title: Dr. Reddy’s Q1 net plunges 69% to ₹435.6 cr on lower revenue
items.summary: Lower sales of cancer drug lenalidomide and ₹239.7 crore provision post recent quality issue around Semaglutide impact revenue of generic drugmaker

items.title: RBI sold $6.1 bn in May as oil weighed on the rupee and foreign inflows showed signs of revival: Bulletin
items.summary: The Reserve Bank of India sold six point one billion dollars in May.  This intervention occurred amid global market volatility and geopolitical tensions.  India's external sector remains steady with improving foreign investment inflows.  The domestic economy navigated uncertainties well, supported by strong demand.  Foreign exchange reserves are comfortable, covering over ten months of imports.

items.title: GreenLine Mobility, Dabur join hands for green logistics solutions
items.summary: GreenLine to deploy its LNG-powered trucks across Dabur’s logistics operations
